mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.5%, consumer spending collapses and corporate earnings forecasts are downgraded, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of periods where UNRATE exceeds 9.5%, or the signal loses predictive power with p-value above 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with a stable or declining federal funds rate indicates strong consumer demand supported by accommodative policy, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS growth exceeds 2% monthly while FEDFUNDS remains flat, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals improving supply-side capacity utilization and corporate output growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week during economic recoveries.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative or flat 5-day forward returns during recovery phases, or the mechanism predictive accuracy drops below random chance in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and rising household income, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple market cycles, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 215.0, inflation expectations become unanchored and force central banks to tighten policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in over 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 215.0, or the threshold effect disappears after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts alongside falling unemployment indicate synchronized strength in consumer confidence and labor market health, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined HOUST and UNRATE signal fails to predict positive returns when housing starts rise but unemployment remains above 8%, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth signals robust aggregate demand and corporate profit expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: GDPC1 acceleration predicts negative 60-day returns during late-cycle phases, or the signal out-of-sample R-squared falls below 0.05 in a rolling 4-year window.
